title: Two gate-wiring blind spots: ship-gate attestation READER has no writer↔reader binding test (stale attestation would be accepted), and evalgate.qualityGate can be removed from NewReviewer without any test failing
summary: (1) commitgate writes attestation.json (commitgate.go:194-200, mutation-verified strong: gremlins 24 killed/0 survived in-package) but the ENFORCER in phases/ship/commitgate.go has no test binding the pair: nothing pins that a stale tree_state_sha is rejected or that a missing/corrupt attestation blocks ship — the two packages are only tested in isolation. (2) evalgate/gates_test.go:111-160 tests qualityGate{}.check() directly, never through NewReviewer().Review(); TestFloorBindingGate_WiredIntoReviewer exists (floorbinding_test.go:219) but has no qualityGate equivalent — deleting qualityGate from the reviewer's gates slice passes the whole suite, silently re-admitting tautological evals (the exact defect the gate was built for).
root_cause: Per-package coverage discipline (apicover) verifies units; nothing forces cross-package/wiring binding tests for gate enforce paths — mutation testing within one package cannot see a severed wire.
fix: Test-only cycle: (1) TestShipGate_StaleAttestationBlocked — write attestation for tree SHA X, mutate a file (tree Y), invoke the ship-gate reader, assert block; twins: fresh attestation passes, missing attestation blocks. (2) TestQualityGate_WiredIntoReviewer mirroring the floor-binding pattern + TestNewReviewer_TautologyEvalBlocksAtEnforce end-to-end. No production changes expected; if the stale-attestation test FAILS as red against current code semantics, that graduates to a P0 production fix in the same cycle.
acceptance: RED-first: both wiring tests written and observed failing via temporary severing (comment-out gate / bypass reader) then green against real wiring
Stale-attestation, missing-attestation, and fresh-attestation triangle all pinned
